title: digitalis purpurea subsp. nevadensis
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Rocky Mountain foxglove, is a subspecies of Digitalis purpurea, a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The medicinal properties of this plant are derived from its foxglove leaves, which contain cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, that have been used to treat various heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea subsp. nevadensis include its ability to strengthen the heart, improve blood circulation, and reduce symptoms of heart failure, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and using them in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although its use should be carefully monitored due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
